title: Senator asks Noem to face U.S. citizens detained by immigration agents
author: CBS News
fullText: Thanks, Mr. Chairman. Thank you for being here today. Madam Secretary, uh for a year you maintain that no US citizens have been arrested or detained by ICE or CBP. After hearings that I conducted in the permanent subcommittee on investigation as the ranking member, you admitted finally in a letter written to me uh just last month that in fact US citizens have been detained and arrested. I'm going to ask, Mr. Chairman, that that letter be entered into the record. >> Thank you. You put the number as 38. Far more American citizens have been arrested by ICE and CBP, probably in the hundreds, perhaps the thousands. Have you met with any of the American citizens who have been detained or arrested by your agency? uh the individuals that may have been detained and arrested um were individuals that could have been obstructing law enforcement operations and committing crimes that way and that we would like to introduce you to until their identify was confirmed. >> Uh Leo, Javiar and Marramar, would you please stand? These three individuals, Madame Secretary, were arrested by your agency, Leonardo Garcia Venegas, Javar Ramirez, and Marramar Martinez. Uh, do you know what your agents did to Leo Venegas? I'll tell you. On May 21 of last year, they entered the private property at a house that he was constructing without consent, without a warrant. illegally again on June 12th. They entered private property, a home where he was doing construction. He is a United States citizen born in Florida. They seized him and ignored and disregarded his proof of citizenship. Wouldn't you agree with me that no US citizen simply working lawfully should be arrested >> in law enforcement operations across the country? There are times when US citizens simple yes or no may be arrested or detained until their identity madam secretary US citizens should not be arrested when they are obeying the law they have no criminal record and they are engaged in a lawful activity. uh sir in situations where law enforcement regardless of the agency across the country when there is probable >> let me tell you about Javier Ramirez >> do you know what your agents did to Javier Ramirez he was on his own private property when he was assaulted by mass agents his own property without a warrant without consent they said get him he's Mexican he was violently slammed into the ground while being handcuffed and taken into custody despite telling officers that he is a United States citizen and even showing them his passport. And when he was asked what he was being arrested for, you know what they said? We don't know. Wouldn't you agree that targeting someone just because he is or looks like he is Mexican when he is a United States citizen is wrong. >> Senator, we do not target people based on their race or ethnicity. We've we do targeted operations based on criminal backgrounds and information that we have. >> Madam Secretary, Javier was detained for over four days. He was denied medicine that he needed for severe diabetes. He lost consciousness. He had severe hypoglycemia. >> Wouldn't you agree with me that medical treatment should have been provided to him? >> Senator denied >> medical treatment is provided to individuals in our processing and detention centers. Within 12 hours, they have a medical examination. We get them the prescriptions and medication that they need. They also have a full >> you commit to take action >> and to look into why he was denied medical treatment. >> Yes, I will look into that case specifically for you. Senator >> Marramar Martinez is with us today as well. She's standing right behind you. She was on her way to donate clothing at her church when she came across an unmarked car. The agents sideswiped her car. Three mass agents in camouflage stormed out and one of them pulled out his gun and fired at her moving vehicle, hitting her five times. She almost bled to death. Wouldn't you agree that shooting Miramar Martinez on her way to donate clothing at her church, a United States citizen from Chicago, is wrong. >> Sir, I don't know the situation or the case. I'll look into it to ensure that all >> I'm glad you'll look into it. >> Uh Myar, by the way, was falsely charged with impeding law enforcement, but the case actually fell apart. The judge dismissed it as being trumped up. He dismissed it with prejudice. And in fact, the agent who shot her, I'm not going to name him, but you know who he is, was quoted on social media the day or so afterward. And he said, quote, "I fired five rounds and she had seven holes." Put that in your book, boys. and quote, "Cool. I'm up for another round of f around and find out." Will you join me in condemning that agent? >> Sir, that situation I don't know the details of, but I will look into that. I don't know why you can't join me in saying it was wrong to shoot Miramar, almost cause her death, and then brag about it. Wouldn't you agree to with me that it was wrong? >> Sir, the way that you have portrayed it, it appears to be, but I let me look into the case so I can speak to the specifics of it. >> In fact, apparently, contrary to what you just said, you actually supported the agent who shot Ms. Martinez five times. He's quoted as saying when he was asked, "Everyone has been supportive, including Chief Bobino, Chief Bank, Secretary Gnome, and Elfe himself, referring presumably to President Trump. Is the agent who shot Miss Martinez still on the job? Sir, our law enforcement officers conduct operations every day according to procedures and to training and the experience that they have. When there is an is the when there is something that is not done still on the job carrying a gun, >> I don't know the details. I will find out and get get that information to you. >> Would you agree with me that he shouldn't be on the job carrying? >> I will look into this case and get back to you on the details. I'm not >> I think Americans would find it absolutely terrifying. that that agent is still on the job carrying a gun and the fact that you can't tell me that you will take him off. >> Sir, do you know do you know who Angel Samago is? Do you know recognize that name? >> Uh you know >> this is an individual talking about >> that was murdered illegality here by an illegal alien of rights and trust. The answers are >> it looks like it was an assassination. I haven't heard you say one victim answers today >> from illegal immigrants that are here perpetuating violence to Miss Miramar. >> I'd like to hear you say the name of one of our victims of illegal criminal activity and fight for one of those victims so that we can stop as many of them from being created in the future by people who should never be in this country to begin with. >> Illegal individuals and aliens that are in this country. Martinez were dismissed with prejudice. Let me ask you about a memo that evidently was written by the acting director of ICE. This memo, have you seen it? >> I can't tell what it says. I've seen lots of memos. I see >> Well, this memo by acting director Todd Lions. It's been frequently referenced in the hearing that I conducted by the whistleblower who testified, Brian Schwank, as being the basis for instructing ICE agents. It specifically says that ICE agents should violate the Fourth Amendment and break into people's homes without a judicial warrant. Do you agree with it? I will look at that memo, sir. I can't read it from here, but if it's referencing administrative warrants, that is the proper legal tool that this body has given us to utilize on immigration enforcement operations. Those administrative warrants were provided and affirmed by the Supreme Court and we'll continue to use those. >> Madam Secretary, does Corey Lewendowski have a role in approving contracts and if so, what is that role? >> Uh, his role as a special government employee. Um, and uh, special government employees work for the White House and the administration.
